channel.title: ClaudeSpy Updates
channel.description: Most recent changes with links to updates.

item.title: Version 2.5
item.description:
Version 2.5

Features

  • Smarter completion detection. On supported Macs, Apple Intelligence is now used to confirm whether Claude has genuinely finished working before signaling that a session is done, reducing premature "done" notifications.
  • New keyboard shortcuts. Adjust terminal font size with ⌘+ and ⌘-, and cycle between sessions with ⌘\` and ⌘⇧\`.
  • pi coding-agent support. Sessions run with the pi coding agent are now monitored alongside existing agents.
  • Browser file downloads. The in-app browser can now download files.

Improvements

  • Resizable Edit Prompt overlay. The Edit Prompt overlay can now be resized, and its height grows automatically as you type.
  • Collapsible usage overview. The usage overview cell can now be collapsed on both iOS and macOS to save space.
  • Preserved file tree scroll position. The file tree keeps its scroll position when switching between tabs or sessions.
  • Browser address bar. Address-bar text is now automatically selected on focus, and a dedicated page is shown when a page fails to load.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed the wrong window being auto-selected on first launch when existing sessions were present.
  • Fixed browser address-bar text losing its selection when clicked with the mouse.
  • Fixed remote-host commands being silently dropped after a viewer switched networks.

item.title: Version 2.4
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 2.4

Features

  • External sidecar plugins (Plugin System v2). ClaudeSpy can now be extended with standalone plugins that add monitoring support for additional coding agents beyond the built-in Claude Code and Codex integrations. Plugins report session status, raise attention badges, and handle permission and question prompts. An opencode plugin is included as the first example.
  • Plugin-provided usage telemetry. Plugins can now feed the per-session token, cost, and latency meter, so sessions run by plugin-supported agents (starting with opencode) show the same usage statistics as built-in agents.
  • Notifications on connected Mac viewers. Macs connected as remote viewers now receive session notifications, so alerts are no longer limited to the host machine.
  • Image and PDF diff viewing. The Git workbench can now display diffs for images and PDFs, not just text files.
  • Commit context-menu actions. Commits in the Git History view now offer a context menu with common actions.

Improvements

  • Better emoji search. The emoji picker now uses a keyword-aware search index, matching common synonyms and related terms so emoji are easier to find. The same improved search is available from the command line.
  • Larger New Session popover. The New Session popover is now wider and taller, making it easier to browse projects and options.
  • Remembered layouts for remote sessions. When viewing a session remotely, the workbench layout (open browser tabs and split arrangement) is now saved and restored per folder, matching the behavior already available for local sessions.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed the Dock badge disappearing after the app cycled between menu-bar-only and regular Dock modes.
  • Fixed the session usage meter showing no data when another OpenTelemetry collector was running on the default port; the app's telemetry receiver now uses its own port and binds only to the local machine.

item.title: Version 2.3
item.description:
Features

  • Per-session token and cost telemetry — Claude Code and Codex sessions now display a per-session token meter along with cost, model, latency, and permission/approval-mode indicators, plus milestones for commits and pull requests.
  • End-of-session recap and cost overview — Each session ends with a recap, and a new cost and usage overview breaks spending down per project and per day, accessible from the menu bar.
  • Per-folder workbench layout — The workbench layout (file and browser tabs, split configuration, and sidebar) is now saved and restored independently for each folder.
  • Collapsible Git branch tree — The Git workbench supports a collapsible branch tree, and its state is preserved between sessions.
  • New-session shortcut — Press ⌘N to start a new local session. The previous ⌘E "Open in Editor" shortcut has been removed.

Improvements

  • Higher-fidelity mirrored terminal — The mirrored terminal now renders with truecolor and 256-color support, a UTF-8 locale, and an accessibility-aware cursor for more accurate output.
  • Editor override with consent — When opening prompts in the in-app editor, the app detects conflicts with an existing editor configuration and asks before applying any change, only enabling the override if you opt in.

Bug Fixes

  • Repeated questions are no longer incorrectly shown as already answered.
  • Long session titles are now truncated on iOS instead of bleeding behind the toolbar buttons.
  • Option-Backspace once again deletes a whole word.

item.title: Version 2.2
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 2.2

Features

  • Changed-file badge on the Git tab — The Git tab now displays a badge showing the number of changed files in a session's repository, so you can see at a glance which sessions have uncommitted work.
  • File actions in the Git tab — Rows in the Git tab's Changes list now support a right-click context menu and double-click actions, making it faster to open and work with modified files directly from the app.
  • Question navigation on iOS — When an agent asks multiple questions at once, the iOS question view now includes navigation arrows so you can move between questions without scrolling.

Improvements

  • Quieter Codex sessions with auto-review enabled — When a Codex session's guardian (auto-review) is set to decide on permission requests automatically, the app no longer sends permission notifications or shows approval forms for those requests, reducing unnecessary interruptions.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed terminal rendering so multi-row background highlight bands — both in scrollback history and in the input composer area — are preserved correctly when the mirrored display is re-captured, instead of losing their background color.
  • Fixed `gallager select-session` so it now switches the app's foreground session in addition to selecting it, matching the expected behavior.
  • Fixed an issue where the macOS app could stall at startup while waiting on a local network hostname lookup, improving launch reliability on some machines and networks.

item.title: Version 2.1
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 2.1

Features

  • Git browser tab — A new horizontal tab provides a built-in Git browser, letting you inspect repository state directly within a monitored session without leaving the app.

Bug Fixes

  • Tab navigation shortcuts — `Cmd-Shift-[` and `Cmd-Shift-]` now move between tabs in their displayed order after tabs have been reordered, so cycling matches what you see on screen.
  • iOS notification badge — The app-icon badge on iOS now clears correctly when a session's attention state is resolved through the agent flow, preventing a lingering badge after items have been handled.

item.title: Version 1.32
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.32

Features

  • Codex CLI support: Added the OpenAI Codex CLI as a second supported coding agent alongside Claude Code. Codex projects are now detected, tagged on iOS, and launch with the Codex CLI when opened.
  • Project scanner: The project scanner no longer requires a `.claude` directory, allowing more projects to be discovered automatically.
  • Emoji lookup: The `gallager set-emoji` command now supports looking up emojis by name or description.

Improvements

  • Sidebar spacing: Added breathing room between the sidebar status icon and emoji badge for better readability.
  • Sparkle updated to 2.9.2.

Bug Fixes

  • Codex "Working" status visible: Fixed an issue where the Codex "Working" status indicator was invisible due to tmux's pane background cache not being pre-warmed.
  • OSC 8 file links: Clicks outside a hyperlink's text no longer trigger the link to open.

item.title: Version 1.31
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.31

Features

  • New browser tab support: Links with `target="_blank"` now open as new browser tabs instead of replacing the current view.
  • Remote tab bar parity: Split, the + menu, and drag-to-reorder are now available in the remote tab bar, matching the local experience.
  • Arrow-key navigation: The new-session project picker now supports keyboard arrow navigation.
  • Pending session indicator: Sessions awaiting attention in the menu bar now use the accent color and a wave icon for better visibility.

Improvements

  • Host name in decryption failures: Notifications about decryption failures now display the originating host name to help identify the source.
  • Silent badge updates: When a host clears its needs-attention state, the badge updates silently without triggering a visible notification.
  • Push notification feedback: Push notifications now play the default sound and trigger haptic feedback.
  • Split pane terminal sizing: The terminal now resizes to fit the width of split panes correctly.
  • Session selection on launch: New local sessions are now selected appropriately even when a remote session was last interacted with.
  • File browser refresh: The file tree now reloads when a watcher attaches, so newly added files appear without manual refresh.
  • Smoother progress animation: The progress animation has been refined for a better visual experience.

Bug Fixes

  • Cursor-position leak: Fixed an issue where DECXCPR cursor-position responses could leak into the tmux pane.
  • Start directory honored: `gallager apply` now correctly honors the `start_directory` of the first pane.
  • Toolbar accessibility on macOS 26: Corrected the accessibility label for toolbar icon buttons on macOS 26.
  • Window filtering: Window resize and move operations now filter to standard windows and retry as needed for improved reliability.

item.title: Version 1.30
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.30

Features

  • Draggable tabs and reordering: Tabs in the main window can now be reordered by dragging. New keyboard shortcuts ⌘⇧[ and ⌘⇧] move between tabs.
  • Browser tab: A new browser tab opens links from the terminal directly inside Gallager, with per-domain decisions to remember whether you want a domain to open in-app or in your default browser.
  • Split-tab view: File and browser tabs can be displayed side-by-side in a split layout.
  • Open files in external editors: File-browser entries can be opened in your preferred external editor.
  • Full-text search in file explorer: Search file contents directly from the file browser.
  • Drag files out of the file explorer: File-browser rows can be dragged into other applications.
  • Drop files from Finder into terminals: Dropping files from Finder onto a terminal pane forwards them to the tmux paste buffer.
  • Image paste forwarding: Images pasted into a Mac viewer are now forwarded to the remote host.
  • Remote link handling on iOS: Links clicked in remote sessions now open inside the app.
  • `gallager set-progress` CLI: New command for driving the sidebar progress indicator, including an `indeterminate` state.
  • `gallager notify --push`: Notifications can now be sent as push notifications.

Improvements

  • Cmd-W scoping: Cmd-W now properly closes the Settings and About windows when they're focused, instead of only acting on terminal panes.
  • File-browser selection: The file-browser list keeps its first-responder state so the selection highlight stays visible.
  • Native context menus: File-browser context menus now use native NSMenu for better platform integration.
  • Sidebar progress alignment: The sidebar progress bar sits slightly lower for better visual alignment.
  • Disk-full alert threshold: The disk-full warning now triggers at 85% usage instead of 80%, reducing false alarms.
  • Sparkle updater: Updated to Sparkle 2.9.1.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed `gallager apply` failing with "can't find window: !" in certain configurations.
  • Fixed an issue where pane streaming could spawn multiple readers; each pane now uses a single persistent reader for more reliable output.

item.title: Version 1.29
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.29

Features

  • Custom emoji icons for tmux sessions: Personalize sessions with emoji icons for easier identification at a glance.
  • Appearance picker: New appearance settings on both macOS and iOS, with session-color swatches in menus and a live preview in the prompt editor.
  • One-tap pairing on iOS: When a pairing code is detected in the iOS clipboard, the app now offers a one-tap paste to streamline device setup.
  • Editable iOS device names: Rename your iOS device, and see the updated name reflected on paired hosts.
  • Progress indicators in mirrored panes: OSC 9;4 progress updates from Claude Code are now forwarded through mirrored tmux panes, surfacing task progress in the UI.

Improvements

  • Updated tool model list: Tool models now match the latest Claude Code documentation.
  • Faster project list refresh: The Claude project list on the host now refreshes every minute automatically.
  • ProjectNavigator upgraded to 1.8.0: Includes the latest improvements from the underlying navigation component.

Bug Fixes

  • URL detection in terminal: URL matching no longer extends into uninitialized terminal cells, preventing spurious or overly long link selections.

item.title: Version 1.28
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.28

Features

  • Per-session colors: Assign a custom color to each session, shown as a dot in the sidebar for quick visual identification.
  • Declarative session layouts: Define and apply tmux session layouts via `gallager apply` for reproducible workspace setups.
  • Host-pressure alerts: Get notified when the relay host is under resource pressure that could affect connectivity.
  • Per-session progress bars: OSC 9;4 progress sequences are now tracked per session.
  • Setup hook event: Support for the new Setup hook event from the Claude Code hooks specification.

Improvements

  • Streamlined iOS settings: Settings have moved from a dedicated tab to a toolbar button, freeing up tab bar space for primary navigation.
  • Smarter Cmd+V and Cmd+C: Copy and paste shortcuts now route only to the focused pane instead of being broadcast.
  • Shift+Enter passthrough: Shift+Enter is now passed through to the terminal for multi-line input in supported tools.
  • CLI ergonomics: The `gallager` CLI gains pane titles, idempotent `create`, `wait-ready`, `send --enter`, and `capture-pane`. Commands now default to the calling pane when no target is specified.
  • Editor scroll position preserved: Scroll position in the in-app editor is retained when switching tabs or sessions.
  • Return to originating terminal: Closing a file tab now returns focus to the terminal you opened it from.
  • Updated tool models: Tool definitions have been updated to match the latest Claude Code documentation.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where iOS terminal link underlines could briefly appear in mouse mode.
  • Fixed double-spacing on mirror attach when the terminal width was narrower than the source pane.
  • Fixed a flake in the Mark Handled action by serializing viewer sends and restoring the home directory correctly.
  • Fixed tmux control clients not disconnecting cleanly during shutdown.
  • Fixed the relay server healthcheck.

item.title: Version 1.27
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.27

Features

  • Sidebar label overrides via CLI: A new `gallager session-state` command lets you customize sidebar labels for tmux sessions from the terminal.
  • Shared file context menu: The file navigator and editor tabs now share a unified context menu, providing consistent file actions across both surfaces.

Improvements

  • Window title reflects selected session: The macOS window title now shows the primary sidebar label of the currently selected session, making it easier to identify which session a window belongs to.
  • Cleaner terminal behavior in mouse mode: Link underlines and clicks are now suppressed while the terminal is in mouse mode, preventing accidental link activation when interacting with mouse-aware applications.

Bug Fixes

  • Clean tmux teardown on quit: Gallager now tears down the tmux `pipe-pane` cleanly when quitting, preventing lingering pipe processes after the app closes.

item.title: Version 1.26
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.26

Features

  • Markdown file suggestion bar: A new suggestion bar makes it easy to open the markdown file currently referenced in the terminal.
  • Open in New Tab: Added an "Open in New Tab" context menu action to the file explorer for quicker navigation between files.
  • File link handling: Clicking `file://` links in the terminal now opens them in a new tab instead of leaving the terminal.
  • New hook events: Added support for `UserPromptExpansion` and `PostToolBatch` hook events, expanding visibility into Claude Code activity.

Improvements

  • iOS drag-to-scroll: Drag-to-scroll now works on iOS when the host session has tmux mouse mode enabled, making it easier to navigate scrollback on touch devices.
  • AskUserQuestion keystrokes: Fixed keystroke handling so remote input correctly drives Claude Code's AskUserQuestion prompt UI.
  • Custom Claude command path preserved: Plugin setup no longer overwrites a user-configured custom path to the `claude` command.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed a background color bleed in terminal rendering that could leave stray styling after capture-pane rebuilds.
  • Fixed an issue where the terminal could appear blank after closing a sibling tmux window.

item.title: Version 1.25
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.25

Features

  • Split pane controls in remote sessions (macOS): Added split pane buttons to remote sessions on macOS, making it easier to manage tmux pane layouts directly from the remote view.

Bug Fixes

  • Blank terminal after closing tab-bar window: Resolved an issue where the terminal could appear blank after closing a tab-bar window.
  • Tab-bar "+" button failures: Fixed an error where the "+" tab-bar button would fail with an "index N in use" message when creating new tabs.
  • Build warnings: Cleaned up unused-value build warnings in the server feature module.

Improvements

  • Streamlined release process: TestFlight "What to Test" notes are now set automatically and chained from the release script, reducing manual steps when shipping new builds.

item.title: Version 1.24
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.24

Features

  • New CLI commands: Added `list-projects` and `start-project` commands to the `gallager` CLI, letting you enumerate and launch projects directly from the terminal.
  • Plugin status per Claude folder: Each custom Claude folder now displays whether the ClaudeSpy plugin is installed, making it easier to see which folders are wired up for monitoring.

Improvements

  • Guided plugin setup: When you add a custom Claude folder, the app now offers to install the ClaudeSpy plugin into it, streamlining initial configuration.
  • Custom folder environment: Projects launched from a custom `.claude` folder now have `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` set automatically, so Claude Code uses the correct configuration for that folder.
  • Version compatibility enforcement: Connections between the Mac host and iOS viewer are now rejected when either side is running an incompatible version, with a clear message instead of a silent mismatch or subtle failure.

item.title: Version 1.23
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.23

Features

  • Editable tmux window names — Tab bars now allow renaming tmux windows directly, without dropping to the terminal.
  • gallager CLI skill — A new skill integrates the `gallager` command-line tool, with an optional zsh completion installer for tab-complete support.

Improvements

  • Smart plugin install prompt — The app now checks whether the `claude` binary is present before offering to install the plugin, avoiding a confusing prompt on systems where Claude Code is not installed.
  • Session-level custom descriptions — Custom pane descriptions are now persisted per session, so they survive reconnects and session switches.
  • Mouse mode sync on stream start — Remote viewers now receive the correct mouse mode state immediately when a stream begins, rather than waiting for the next mode change.
  • Pane state isolated by host — Viewer pane state is now keyed by both pair ID and pane ID, preventing state collisions when monitoring panes across multiple hosts.
  • Improved log visibility — OSLog messages from the app are no longer double-redacted, making diagnostics easier to read in Console.

Bug Fixes

  • Generate Pair button and toolbar disconnect — Fixed a bug where the Generate Pair button and the confirm toolbar did not behave correctly in certain states.
  • File explorer refresh — The file explorer now correctly updates when new files are created in a watched directory.
  • Code editor reliability — Fixed several issues in the code editor: text was not always persisted, key events were not forwarded correctly, and terminal focus was not restored after editing.
  • Text selection in markdown viewer — Text can now be selected and copied from the markdown file viewer.

item.title: Version 1.22
item.description:
Release Notes - Version 1.22

New Features

  • Gallager CLI: A new cmux-compatible command-line API is now available, enabling scripted interaction and programmatic control of Claude Code sessions.
  • Remote Clipboard Sync: Text copied on the remote host is now synced to the viewer automatically via OSC 52, streamlining clipboard transfer between machines.
  • Close Tmux Windows and Sessions Remotely: Remote viewers can now close tmux windows and sessions directly from the iOS app.
  • Desktop Download Guidance: The iOS pairing screen now displays instructions for downloading the desktop app, making first-time setup easier.

Improvements

  • Streamlined iOS Terminal Toolbar: Terminal toolbar commands on iOS have been consolidated into a single menu popover, reducing visual clutter and providing a more organized command layout.

Bug Fixes

  • Terminal Rendering: Resolved an issue where SGR underline styling could leak across terminal content during mirror re-capture, ensuring correct text formatting.
  • E2EE Tests: Fixed a build failure caused by an ambiguous `CryptoError` reference in the end-to-end encryption test suite.

item.title: Version 1.21
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.21 Release Notes

Features

  • Smart window close behavior — Closing a window tab with Cmd-W now intelligently closes just the active tab instead of the entire window. A dedicated close button has also been added to each tab. (#339)
  • Tmux installation guide — A guided setup flow now appears at startup if tmux is not installed, helping new users get up and running quickly. (#337)
  • Multi-folder project discovery — ClaudeSpy now scans multiple `.claude` directories, improving automatic discovery of Claude Code projects across your system. (#336)

Improvements

  • Text selection in mouse-mode terminals — Synthesized SGR drag sequences fix text selection when terminal mouse mode is active, making it possible to select and copy text reliably. (#334)
  • Simplified Claude path settings — Removed the legacy ClaudePathDetector in favor of a streamlined configuration, reducing complexity and potential misconfiguration.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed session state loss from transient tmux errors — Temporary tmux communication failures no longer wipe the active Claude session state, preventing unexpected resets during monitoring. (#342)
  • Fixed needsAttention badge in yolo mode — The attention indicator now correctly clears when yolo mode auto-approves a permission request, eliminating stale notifications. (#340)
  • Fixed iOS TestFlight upload — Removed the alpha channel from the iOS app icon, resolving an issue that prevented successful submission to TestFlight.

item.title: Version 1.20
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.20 Release Notes

Features

  • File Search in File Browser — The file browser sidebar now includes a search field, making it easy to find files quickly without manually scrolling through the directory tree.
  • Subagent Permission Hooks — Permission request hooks now fire for subagent tool calls in addition to the main agent, giving you visibility into all permission-related activity during a session.

Improvements

  • Updated App Icons and Accent Color — Fresh app icons and a new accent color across both macOS and iOS.
  • Cleaner File Explorer — The redundant root node has been removed from the file explorer, so you jump straight into the project contents.
  • Better Plugin Error Reporting — When a plugin fails to install, the app now shows a detailed error message with a copyable report, making it easier to diagnose and share issues.

Bug Fixes

  • Project List Sorting — The project list now correctly sorts by most recently used, so your active projects appear at the top.
  • Welcome Screen Layout — Fixed layout clipping and text truncation on the welcome screen that could cut off content on smaller windows.
  • Build Script Fix — Fixed the TestFlight build script reading the version number from the wrong configuration file.

item.title: Version 1.19
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.19 Release Notes

Features

  • In-app prompt editing with Ctrl-G — Press Ctrl-G to capture and edit prompts directly within the app, powered by the new GallagerEditor integration.
  • Git branch in sidebar — The current git branch is now displayed in the sidebar, giving you immediate visibility into which branch each session is working on.
  • Auto-close terminal pane on exit — New option to automatically close the terminal pane when a Claude Code session ends, keeping your workspace tidy.
  • Auto-approve on yolo mode — Pending permission requests are now automatically approved when you switch to yolo (auto-approve) mode, so you don't have to manually clear the queue.
  • PermissionDenied hook — A new hook fires when a permission request is denied, enabling custom automation workflows around denied actions.
  • Mouse support for remote sessions — Remote viewers can now use mouse input, bringing the remote monitoring experience closer to parity with local sessions.
  • Detect running sessions on startup — ClaudeSpy now detects already-running Claude Code instances when the app launches, so you no longer need to restart sessions for them to appear.

Improvements

  • Faster remote typing — Keystrokes are now batched before being sent to the relay server, significantly improving responsiveness when typing in remote sessions.
  • Removed standalone mirror window — The legacy standalone mirror window feature has been removed, simplifying preferences and reducing maintenance surface.
  • Updated setting defaults — Default settings have been refreshed to better reflect common usage patterns.
  • Viewer sessions cleared on host disconnect — When a host disconnects, remote viewer sessions are now properly cleaned up.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed jumpy trackpad scrolling — Resolved an issue where scrolling with a trackpad in terminal mouse mode would jump erratically.
  • Fixed terminal title for remote viewers — The terminal title is now correctly sent to remote viewers on their initial connection.
  • Fixed permission request attention state — Permission requests no longer lose their "Needs attention" indicator prematurely.
  • Fixed GallagerEditor notarization — Resolved a build phase warning and notarization issue with the GallagerEditor component.
  • Fixed redundant Void return — Cleaned up a minor code issue in FileSystemLoadingService.

item.title: Version 1.18
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.18 Release Notes

Features

  • Customizable sidebar cell layout — You can now configure how session cells appear in the sidebar, tailoring the layout to show the information most relevant to you. (#287)
  • File browser tab — A new file browser is available as a horizontal tab alongside the terminal view, letting you browse project files directly within the app. (#263)

Improvements

  • Async plugin hooks — All plugin hooks now run asynchronously, preventing them from blocking Claude Code while processing events. This results in a smoother experience when hooks fire during active sessions. (#288)
  • Subagent hook filtering — Hook events that fire inside Claude Code subagents are now ignored, reducing noise and ensuring only top-level session events are tracked. (#286)
  • Idle prompt notification filtering — The idle prompt terminal notification from Claude Code is now filtered out, keeping your terminal view clean of transient system messages. (#292)

Bug Fixes

  • Incorrect "needs attention" state — Fixed an issue where idle prompt notifications would incorrectly trigger the "needs attention" indicator on sessions, causing false alerts. (#283)

item.title: Version 1.17
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.17 Release Notes

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed stuck "needs attention" indicator on sessions. Previously, the attention badge on a session could remain visible even after viewing the session, if the most recent event was a notification, stop failure, or elicitation. The indicator now correctly clears for all event types.
  • Attention indicator now auto-clears on macOS when viewing the active session. If you already have a session selected and a new event arrives for that session, the attention state is automatically dismissed without requiring you to navigate away and back.

item.title: Version 1.16
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.16 Release Notes

Features

  • Horizontal window tabs in remote sessions — Remote viewer sessions now display tmux window tabs along the top of the terminal, matching the local session experience. Switch between windows or create new ones directly from the tab bar. (#274)
  • Mouse support in terminal — Terminal views now support mouse input including clicks, scrolling, and tracking. Applications running in fullscreen mode within Claude Code can now receive full mouse interactivity. (#269)

Improvements

  • Persistent viewer reconnection — The Mac viewer now retries reconnection indefinitely with intelligent backoff when a remote host goes offline, instead of entering a permanent error state. (#277)
  • Simplified launch behavior — The "restore windows on launch" setting has been replaced with a more reliable "open panes window on launch" option, giving you direct control over whether the panes window appears at startup. (#268)
  • Updated tool definitions — Internal tool models have been updated to match the current Claude Code hooks API, including new tools like ToolSearch and Skill, and removal of deprecated ones. (#265)

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed crash during process launch — Resolved a SIGABRT crash caused by an unhandled Objective-C exception when launching terminal processes with invalid paths or other failure conditions. (#279)
  • Fixed stuck "needs attention" indicator — The attention badge now correctly clears after approving a permission request, for both local and remote viewers. (#272)

item.title: Version 1.15
item.description:
I have enough context now. Here are the release notes: # ClaudeSpy 1.15

Features

Window Tabs for Multi-Window Sessions

Sessions with multiple tmux windows now display horizontal tabs above the terminal view, making it easy to switch between windows without navigating back to the sidebar.
  • macOS: A tab bar appears above the terminal with clickable tabs for each window, plus a "+" button to create new windows directly from the app.
  • iOS: Tap the navigation title to open a menu listing all windows in the session, with an option to create new windows.
  • The sidebar now groups windows by session, showing one entry per session instead of individual window entries.
When selecting a session, the app now correctly opens the tmux-active window rather than always defaulting to the first window.

Improvements

  • Smarter window close handling: Closing a tab automatically selects another window in the same session instead of showing an empty state.
  • iOS Claude status accuracy: The session list now correctly shows Claude activity status even when Claude is running on a non-active pane.
  • Tall terminal scroll fix: Terminals with more rows than the screen now scroll to the top on both macOS and iOS, keeping the prompt visible instead of hiding it above the viewport.
  • Cross-platform window sync: Switching windows on iOS is reflected on macOS, and vice versa.

item.title: Version 1.14
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.14 Release Notes

Features

  • Better tmux integration — Send split, select, and prefix commands directly from ClaudeSpy, giving you more control over your tmux sessions without switching context.
  • Custom window descriptions with cross-device sync — Label your tmux windows with custom descriptions that sync between your Mac and iOS devices.
  • "Always auto-resize terminals" preference — New setting to automatically resize terminal mirrors to match pane dimensions, without needing to toggle it per session.
  • 11 new hook event types — Support for additional Claude Code hook events, enabling richer monitoring of session activity.
  • Context menu on session sidebar — Right-click (or long-press on iOS) session rows in the sidebar for quick actions.
  • iOS pane grouping by tmux window — Panes on iOS are now grouped by their parent tmux window, matching the macOS sidebar layout.

Improvements

  • Smarter notification handling — Notifications are automatically marked as handled when you view a session, and low-value notifications have been removed to reduce noise.
  • Event-driven session working status — The "working" indicator for Claude sessions now updates via events rather than polling, improving responsiveness and efficiency.
  • Kitty keyboard protocol support — Terminal input sequences from the Kitty keyboard protocol are now correctly stripped and parsed, improving compatibility with modern terminal emulators.
  • SwiftTerm updated to 1.13.0 — Updated the embedded terminal engine for better rendering and compatibility.
  • Unit tests integrated into CI and release pipelines — Automated test coverage now runs on every build and release.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed missing footer on iOS for tall terminals — The bottom toolbar no longer disappears when viewing sessions with tall terminal output on iOS.
  • Fixed scrollback gap after status updates — Resolved an issue where terminal status updates could destroy content, leaving gaps in the scrollback buffer.
  • Fixed "clear" text appearing at top of mirror terminal — The literal text "clear" no longer shows at the top of mirrored terminal output after a screen clear.
  • Fixed scrollback regression from SwiftTerm update — Restored correct scrollback behavior that was broken during the SwiftTerm 1.12.0 upgrade.

item.title: Version 1.13
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.13 Release Notes

Features

  • Tmux windows in sidebar — The sidebar now displays tmux windows instead of individual panes, providing a cleaner and more intuitive navigation experience when monitoring multiple sessions.
  • Pending badge on dock and app switcher icons — The macOS dock icon and app switcher now show a badge when there are pending notifications, making it easier to notice activity at a glance.
  • Persistent terminal titles — Terminal titles are now preserved when switching between panes and are detected even on inactive panes, so you always see the correct title regardless of which pane is selected.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed escape sequence leaks in notifications — Terminal escape sequences are now properly sanitized before appearing in notification content, preventing garbled text in alerts.
  • Fixed SwiftTerm DA responses appearing as typed input — Device attribute responses from the terminal emulator no longer leak through as if they were user-typed input.

Internal Improvements

  • Unified pane state management — Per-pane state has been consolidated into a single `PaneState` struct, improving consistency and reliability of pane tracking.

item.title: Version 1.12
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.12 Release Notes

Features

  • Rich text copy from terminal — Copy terminal selections as formatted rich text (Cmd+Option+C) preserving colors, bold, italic, and other styling. Also available: copy with ANSI escape sequences (Ctrl+Cmd+C) for pasting into other terminals.
  • Auto-copy on select — New option to automatically copy selected terminal text to the clipboard when the selection is released.

Improvements

  • Cleaner iOS experience in yolo mode — Permission requests that are auto-approved on the Mac (such as file reads) no longer show response UI or trigger push notifications on iOS. Events requiring manual input still appear as expected.
  • Yolo mode persists across context compaction — Yolo mode is no longer reset when Claude Code compacts context mid-session. It now carries over seamlessly and only clears on an explicit session end.
  • Improved terminal text copy — Plain text copy now trims trailing whitespace that previously padded each line to the full column width.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed box drawing misalignment on non-Retina displays — Table borders and box-drawing characters no longer drift out of alignment with text content on standard-resolution screens.
  • Fixed emoji and wide character column alignment — Tables containing emoji or CJK characters now render with correct column alignment. Wide characters are properly counted as two columns during terminal processing.
  • Updated SwiftTerm to 1.11.2 — Fixes upstream issues with emoji background gaps, flag emoji rendering, and Unicode width detection.

item.title: Version 1.11
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.11 Release Notes

Features

  • Terminal Notifications (OSC 9/777) — ClaudeSpy now captures and displays terminal notification sequences, so you can see alerts and status updates from your Claude Code sessions remotely on iOS.
  • Yolo Mode — A new per-session option to auto-approve permission prompts, letting Claude Code run uninterrupted. Approvals are sent as an Enter keypress with a 500ms delay for reliability.

Bug Fixes

  • Table and box-drawing characters — Fixed an issue where box-drawing characters (used in tables, borders, and TUI layouts) rendered as plain ASCII in the mirrored terminal view. These now display correctly.
  • Cursor style changes — The mirrored terminal now properly reflects cursor style updates (block, underline, bar) sent via the DECSCUSR escape sequence.
  • Semicolon keystrokes — Fixed semicolons not being delivered to the tmux pane when typing commands remotely.

Internal Improvements

  • E2E test baseline cleanup — Removed numeric prefixes from end-to-end test baseline folders for cleaner organization.

item.title: Version 1.10
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.10

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed "File exists" error when starting terminal mirroring. Resolved an issue where stale FIFO files left behind from a previous crash or unclean shutdown could prevent pipe-pane streaming from starting. The app now correctly detects and removes leftover files before retrying, eliminating the need to manually clean up temporary files.
  • Improved temporary file handling across user accounts. FIFO pipes are now created in the system's per-user temporary directory instead of a shared location, preventing potential permission conflicts when multiple user accounts run ClaudeSpy on the same machine.

item.title: Version 1.9
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.9 Release Notes

Features

Terminal Title Display

Terminal titles set by programs via standard escape sequences (OSC 0/2) are now detected and displayed throughout the app. Titles appear in the macOS sidebar, mirror windows, the remote macOS viewer, and the iOS viewer, making it easier to identify what each terminal session is running.

Clickable Hyperlinks in Terminals

Terminals now detect and render clickable hyperlinks emitted via OSC 8 escape sequences, in addition to the existing plain-text URL detection. On macOS, links are underlined on hover and open on click. On iOS, links respond to tap and long-press gestures. When both an OSC 8 link and a plain-text URL overlap the same range, the escape sequence link takes priority.

Session Summaries

When a Claude Code session ends, the app now captures and displays Claude's final message as a session summary. On iOS, the summary appears as a collapsible section above the prompt input. On macOS, it shows as a subtitle in the sidebar. Session summaries are also included in push notifications, giving a quick overview of what was accomplished without needing to open the app.

Improvements

Rewritten Terminal Streaming Architecture

Live terminal data delivery has been completely rewritten. The previous approach parsed tmux control mode `%output` events, which required octal unescaping, UTF-8 reconstruction, and line-boundary splitting — all of which could introduce rendering artifacts, especially with truecolor animations. The new architecture uses `pipe-pane` to deliver raw PTY bytes through per-pane FIFOs, eliminating these processing layers and producing faithful terminal output.

Bug Fixes

Sidebar Scroll Position After Session Exit

Fixed an issue where sidebar elements could become hidden off-screen when a Claude session ended. The pane would move from "Claude Sessions" to "Terminals" without adjusting the scroll position, causing items to disappear from view.

Keystroke Ordering in Remote Viewers

Fixed a race condition where rapid keystrokes sent from a remote viewer (iOS or macOS) could arrive out of order at the host terminal. Each keystroke previously spawned an independent task that could overtake earlier sends; keystrokes are now serialized to guarantee correct ordering.

item.title: Version 1.8
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.8 Release Notes

Features

  • Remote sessions in menu bar — Connected remote sessions now appear directly in the menu bar, making it easier to see active monitoring at a glance. A new panes view option provides an alternative way to browse terminal panes.

Bug Fixes

  • Terminal rendering improvements — Fixed several terminal rendering issues including incorrect escape sequence handling, cursor positioning errors, and capture timing. These fixes result in more accurate and reliable terminal mirroring.
  • Sidebar selection and scrolling — Fixed an issue where sidebar selection and scroll position could jump unexpectedly when panes moved between sections.
  • Shift-Tab key forwarding — Fixed Shift-Tab not being forwarded to tmux sessions, restoring proper keyboard navigation in monitored terminals.

Internal Improvements

  • Expanded end-to-end test coverage, including two-Mac pairing scenarios and empty state flows.
  • Replaced the in-app test server with an external accessibility client for more reliable automated testing.
  • Improved dependency injection patterns across the codebase.

item.title: Version 1.7
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.7 Release Notes

Improvements

  • Updated plugin naming — The Claude Code plugin has been renamed for consistency. If you previously installed the plugin, it will be recognized under its new name automatically.
  • Release rollback support — The release process now supports rollback, making it easier to revert to a previous version if an issue is discovered after publishing an update.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed remaining references to old plugin name — Resolved an issue where some internal references still used the previous plugin name, which could cause the plugin to not be detected correctly.

item.title: Version 1.6
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy v1.6 Release Notes

Features

  • Project Search — Added a search field to project selection views, making it easier to find projects when you have many Claude Code sessions.
  • New Session Button — An empty sidebar now shows a "New Session" button, providing a clearer path to get started.
  • Resize Pane to Fit — New toolbar button to resize the tmux pane to match the mirror view dimensions, eliminating layout mismatches.
  • LSP Tools Support — Language Server Protocol tools now work correctly within monitored sessions.

Improvements

  • App Renamed to Gallager — The macOS app has been renamed from ClaudeSpy to Gallager.
  • Unlimited Reconnection — The Mac app now retries server connections indefinitely instead of giving up after 10 attempts, improving reliability for long-running sessions.
  • Terminal Cleared on Session End — The remote terminal view on iOS now clears when a session ends, preventing stale output from lingering.
  • Service Architecture — Migrated 8 internal services to a dependency injection pattern, improving testability and maintainability.
  • Unpairing Notifications — Other services are now properly notified when a device is unpaired.
  • E2E Testing Infrastructure — Added screenshot comparison, test report generation, Claude session hook scenarios, and improved test isolation from the production app.

Bug Fixes

  • Duplicate View Menu — Fixed an issue where the macOS menu bar displayed two "View" menus.
  • Keychain Updates — Resolved a bug where keychain values were not being updated correctly.
  • E2E Test Reliability — Fixed tmux socket permission errors and window detection issues in the end-to-end test suite.

item.title: Version 1.5
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.5 Release Notes

Features

  • Clickable Links in Terminal Mirrors — URLs displayed in mirrored terminal panes are now highlighted and clickable, making it easier to open links directly from monitored sessions.
  • E2E Test Coordinator with Storage Isolation — A new end-to-end test coordinator enables reliable integration testing with fully isolated storage, improving confidence in the test suite.
  • Signing Credentials Transfer for Multi-Machine Builds — A new script allows transferring code signing credentials between machines, simplifying setup for developers building on multiple Macs.

Improvements

  • Dependency Injection with PointFree Dependencies — Adopted the PointFree Dependencies library for structured dependency injection, improving testability and modularity across the codebase.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed Signing Certificate Selection — The transfer-signing process now correctly filters certificates by team and searches the Xcode provisioning profile directory, resolving issues where the wrong certificate could be selected during builds.

item.title: Version 1.4
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.4 Release Notes

New Features

Mac-to-Mac Viewer Mode

Monitor Claude Code sessions running on remote Macs directly from another Mac. Includes full terminal streaming, a dedicated pairing flow, and viewer UI — extending ClaudeSpy beyond mobile monitoring.

Session Creation from Sidebar

Quickly start new Claude Code sessions directly from the macOS and iOS sidebars, with support for both local and remote hosts.

Multi-Device Pairing

Pair multiple iOS devices with a single Mac app, so your whole team can monitor sessions simultaneously.

Launch at Login

Optionally start ClaudeSpy automatically when you log in, with a first-launch prompt to configure the setting.

Additional Hook Types

Added support for `TeammateIdle`, `TaskCompleted`, and other previously missing hook event types, enabling richer session monitoring and notifications.

Improvements

  • Dynamic port allocation for hook server — Multiple users on the same machine no longer conflict over ports.
  • Sessions grouped by Claude status — The macOS sidebar now organizes sessions by their current status for easier scanning.
  • Username included in Mac-to-iOS data — iOS devices now display which user a session belongs to.
  • Terminal view shown by default — iOS Claude sessions open directly to the terminal view instead of requiring an extra tap.
  • Improved terminal sizing — New sessions on Mac start with better default terminal dimensions.
  • Landscape layout refinements on iOS — Hidden tab bar and reduced navigation bar give more screen space in landscape orientation.
  • Updated SwiftTerm to v1.10.1 — Incorporates upstream terminal emulation fixes.
  • Refactored pairing protocol — Internal improvements to pairing response handling for better reliability.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed Cmd+V paste on macOS terminal — Paste now works reliably in the built-in terminal.
  • Fixed Cmd+Left/Right word movement — These shortcuts now move the cursor by word instead of typing "b" or "f".
  • Fixed menu bar window activation — Clicking the menu bar icon now reliably brings windows to the front.
  • Fixed push notifications not opening sessions — Tapping a notification now correctly navigates to the relevant session.
  • Fixed terminal reconnection getting stuck — Stream reconnections no longer hang on "connecting" indefinitely.
  • Fixed session sync on Mac reconnect — Sessions now sync correctly when the Mac reconnects while iOS is already connected.
  • Fixed iOS new session popover — The popover for creating sessions on the first paired Mac no longer opens blank.
  • Fixed high CPU usage — Resolved an issue that could cause excessive CPU consumption.
  • Fixed iOS compilation — Resolved a build issue affecting the iOS target.

item.title: Version 1.1
item.description:
# ClaudeSpy 1.1 Release Notes

Features

Interactive Terminal Input (macOS)

Terminal mirrors on macOS now support keyboard input, allowing you to interact directly with monitored Claude Code sessions rather than just observing them.

Multi-Mac Server Support (iOS)

The iOS app can now connect to and monitor multiple Mac servers simultaneously, making it easier to keep track of Claude Code sessions running across different machines.

Plugin Installation Guide

First-time users on macOS will now see clear instructions for installing the Claude Code plugin, streamlining the initial setup process.

Bug Fixes

Improved Connection Reliability (macOS)

Fixed an issue where the Mac app would disconnect from the server and fail to automatically reconnect. The app now properly retries connections when they are interrupted.

item.title: Version 1.0
item.description:
What's New in 1.0

  • Initial release with Sparkle auto-update support
